---
title: "LRPITT07: ITT analysis of letter-sound knowledge (YARC-LSK) (L)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import arviz as az
import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
trace = az.from_netcdf(_here / "trace.nc")
tau_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "tau_summary.csv")
rope_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "rope_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPITT07 is the intention-to-treat (ITT) analysis for letter-sound knowledge (YARC-LSK) (`yarclet`). The
estimand is the total effect of randomised group assignment $G$ on the post-score
$L_{\text{post}}$, using the RCT phase only (time 1 $\rightarrow$ time 2).
Letter sounds carried the strongest treatment effect in the earlier joint model.

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so the ITT effect $\tau$ is
identified by the **empty adjustment set**. The own baseline $\gamma_{\text{own}}$
and linear age $\gamma_A$ are **precision terms only** — they sharpen $\tau$ but are
not what licenses the causal interpretation, and no cross-baseline conditioning is
used. Attendance / sessions are never conditioned on (a DAG collider).
**Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means
the intervention raises the outcome.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(L_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i},
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $L_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 32$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.
HSGPs on age and own baseline are off by default (the 2026-04-18 sensitivity
verdict, `notes/202604181445-lrp52-gp-sensitivity.md`).

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Shared priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=32%}
![tau](prior_tau.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=32%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=32%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=32%}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Treatment-effect summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
tau_summary
```

- `tau_logit_*`: posterior mean and equal-tailed central interval (coverage `ci_prob`) of $\tau$ on the logit scale.
- `tau_prob_*`: average marginal effect on the probability scale (risk difference), averaged over the fitted sample per posterior draw.
- `prob_tau_pos`: $P(\tau > 0) = P(\text{intervention helps})$.

## Reporting: direction, magnitude, and practical significance

This follows `notes/202606261304-evidence-strength-and-rope-reporting.md` and the
`METHODS.md` "Interpret" rule: report the **median** effect
(transformation-invariant across the logit and items scales) with intervals, and
separate **direction** (is there a benefit?) from **magnitude** (is it big enough to
matter?). Magnitude is judged against a minimally-important difference $\delta$ —
a region of practical equivalence (ROPE) on the items scale — set per outcome in
`measures.ROPE_DELTA` (provisional, owned by the education lead).

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
r = rope_summary.iloc[0]


def _odds(p):
    p = min(max(float(p), 1e-9), 1 - 1e-9)
    o = p / (1 - p)
    return f"{o:.0f}:1" if o >= 1 else f"1:{1 / o:.0f}"


print(
    f"The intervention raised letter-sound knowledge by a median of "
    f"**{r.items_median:+.1f} items** "
    f"(50% CrI {r.items_lo50:+.1f} to {r.items_hi50:+.1f}; "
    f"95% CrI {r.items_lo:+.1f} to {r.items_hi:+.1f}). "
    f"**Direction** — evidence it helps: "
    f"pd = {r.pd:.3f} ({_odds(r.pd)}, *{r.direction_label} evidence*). "
    f"**Magnitude** — evidence the benefit is at least "
    f"$\\delta$ = {r.delta_items:g} items: "
    f"P = {r.prob_benefit_ge_delta:.3f} "
    f"({_odds(r.prob_benefit_ge_delta)}, *{r.benefit_label} evidence*); "
    f"probability practically negligible (inside the ROPE): {r.prob_in_rope:.3f}."
)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
rope_summary.T
```

![ROPE-anchored posterior of the effect, and the probability of a benefit of at least delta items](rope_summary.png){.lightbox}

::: {.callout-note title="Reading the evidence labels"}

The round-odds labels (*inconclusive / suggestive / moderate / strong / very
strong*) qualify the **evidence**, never the effect *size*, and are run separately
for the direction and the meaningful-benefit claims. They are a last-resort handle
for prose — the odds and probabilities are the real quantities. At this study's
sample size the point estimate is, on average, magnitude-inflated (a Type-M /
winner's-curse effect), so lead with the interval, not the point.

:::

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
